programs.name: West Virginia Medicaid Personal Care Services Program
programs.what_it_does: This in-home care program provides trained direct-care workers to assist with Activities of Daily Living (ADLs) like eating, bathing, and dressing in the home, workplace, or community. A registered nurse evaluates medical eligibility based on ADL needs.[6]
programs.who_its_for: West Virginia Medicaid members needing help in at least three ADLs; seniors/disabled aiming for independent living.[6]
programs.benefit_value: In-home personal care services tailored to assessed ADL deficits; hours based on nurse evaluation.

programs.name: Aged and Disabled Waiver
programs.what_it_does: The Aged and Disabled Waiver is a Home and Community Based Services (HCBS) Medicaid waiver providing long-term care alternatives to nursing homes. It offers services like personal care to help eligible seniors and disabled individuals live in their homes or communities.[5]
programs.who_its_for: Medicaid-eligible seniors aged 65+ or disabled WV residents meeting medical and financial criteria for nursing facility level of care.[5]
programs.benefit_value: Covers HCBS such as personal care, home modifications; prevents or delays institutionalization; value based on assessed needs.

programs.name: Program of All-Inclusive Care for the Elderly (PACE)
programs.what_it_does: PACE provides comprehensive medical and social services to eligible frail seniors, including primary care, hospitalization, prescription drugs, and supportive services to avoid nursing home placement. In WV, availability is limited; no specific WV-branded name identified in results.
programs.who_its_for: Community-dwelling adults 55+ certified for nursing facility level of care, able to live safely at home with services; Medicaid/Medicare eligible.[1]
programs.benefit_value: Full capitation coverage of Medicare/Medicaid benefits plus extras; prevents institutionalization (limited WV programs per general knowledge).

programs.name: Medicare Savings Programs (QMB, SLMB, QI)
programs.what_it_does: These programs help pay Medicare Part A/B premiums, deductibles, coinsurance, and copayments for low-income beneficiaries. Administered via WV Medicaid as Qualified Medicare Beneficiary (QMB), Specified Low-Income Medicare Beneficiary (SLMB), and Qualifying Individual (QI).
programs.who_its_for: Low-income Medicare enrollees aged 65+ meeting WV-specific income/asset limits (e.g., higher thresholds than full Medicaid).[1]
programs.benefit_value: QMB: Full premiums/costs; SLMB: Part B premium; QI: Part B premium; 2026 income limits apply (e.g., ~135-150% FPL).

programs.name: SNAP
programs.what_it_does: The Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program provides monthly benefits via EBT card to buy food for eligible low-income households. Seniors aged 60+ have simplified rules and higher deductions in WV.[7][8]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income WV seniors 60+, disabled, or families meeting income tests (elderly defined as 60+ for SNAP).[8]
programs.benefit_value: Average monthly benefit ~$200-300/person (varies by income/household; max for elderly higher with deductions).

programs.name: LIHEAP
programs.what_it_does: Low Income Home Energy Assistance Program provides grants to help with heating/cooling bills, crisis assistance, and weatherproofing. WV administers federally funded grants seasonally.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income WV households including seniors 60+ meeting income guidelines (up to 150% FPL typically).
programs.benefit_value: One-time grants $200-$1,000+ for heating; crisis aid immediate (varies by fuel type/need).

programs.name: Weatherization Assistance Program (WAP)
programs.what_it_does: WAP funds energy efficiency upgrades like insulation, sealing, and furnace repairs to reduce energy costs and improve home safety. Administered via WV agencies for low-income homes.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income WV households, prioritizing seniors 60+, disabled, with children.
programs.benefit_value: Free home improvements valued $3,000-$8,000 avg.; long-term energy savings 20-30%.

programs.name: SHIP (State Health Insurance Assistance Program)
programs.what_it_does: Free counseling on Medicare options, appeals, and extra help; WV's SHIP provides unbiased assistance via local agencies. Helps seniors choose plans and maximize benefits.
programs.who_its_for: WV Medicare beneficiaries 65+, caregivers, family seeking Medicare guidance.
programs.benefit_value: Free one-on-one counseling; saves avg. $1,000+/year via optimal plan selection.

programs.name: Meals on Wheels
programs.what_it_does: Delivers nutritious meals to homebound seniors to promote health and independence. Provided via WV Area Agencies on Aging like Metro AAA.
programs.who_its_for: Homebound WV seniors 60+ unable to prepare meals, often low-income.
programs.benefit_value: 5-14 meals/week at $3-7/suggested donation; full subsidy for low-income.

programs.name: Caregiver/Respite Support
programs.what_it_does: Provides temporary relief for family caregivers through in-home aides, adult day care, or facility respite. Offered via WV Aged & Disabled Waiver or AAA programs.
programs.who_its_for: Family caregivers of WV seniors/disabled on Medicaid waivers or via local AAAs.
programs.benefit_value: Hourly respite up to 240/year; reduces caregiver burnout (value based on assessed needs).

programs.name: Senior Community Service Employment Program (SCSEP)
programs.what_it_does: Subsidized part-time community service jobs with training for low-income seniors to gain skills and transition to unsubsidized employment. Administered nationally in WV.
programs.benefit_value: 20 hrs/week at min. wage (~$7,000/year); work experience/training.

programs.name: Legal Aid for Seniors
programs.what_it_does: Free legal services for income-eligible seniors on issues like benefits, housing, abuse. Provided by Legal Aid of WV and Senior Legal Aid.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income WV seniors 60+ facing civil legal problems.
programs.benefit_value: Pro bono representation; resolves benefits/housing disputes (saves thousands in costs).

programs.name: Long-Term Care Ombudsman
programs.what_it_does: Advocates for residents in nursing homes/assisted living, investigating complaints and ensuring quality care rights. WV program operates statewide via AAAs.
programs.who_its_for: WV seniors in long-term care facilities, families, staff reporting issues.
programs.benefit_value: Free advocacy/resolution of 1,000s complaints/year; improves facility conditions.

programs.name: Lighthouse Program
programs.what_it_does: The Lighthouse Program provides subsidized in-home care to seniors who are not eligible for Medicaid. Services include help with personal care tasks, mobility, meal preparation, errands, and light housekeeping, up to 60 hours per month.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Seniors aged 60+ who are ineligible for Medicaid but need assistance to remain at home.[1]
programs.whats_unique: State-funded program specifically for non-Medicaid eligible seniors, offering low-cost in-home care not covered by federal programs.[1]
programs.benefit_value: Up to 60 hours of in-home care per month at low cost.[1]

programs.name: Aging Well, Safe at Home Initiative: WV BoSS Home Modification and Accessibility Program
programs.what_it_does: This program funds home modifications and accessibility devices to help seniors live more independently at home. It supports those aged 60+ who demonstrate a need for such improvements.[7]
programs.who_its_for: Seniors aged 60+ needing home modifications for independent living.[4][7]
programs.whats_unique: State-specific initiative through the Bureau of Senior Services (BoSS) providing targeted funds for home accessibility, beyond standard federal aid.[4][7]
programs.benefit_value: Funds for home modifications and accessibility devices (amount based on assessed need).[7]

programs.name: Take Me Home, West Virginia Program
programs.what_it_does: The program assists seniors in transitioning from long-term care facilities back into the community. It provides support to enable safe return and maintenance of home-based living.[4]
programs.who_its_for: Seniors in long-term care facilities ready to transition home.[4]
programs.whats_unique: West Virginia-specific program focused on community re-entry from institutional care, coordinated by local area agencies on aging.[4]
programs.benefit_value: Assistance for transition services (details vary by individual assessment).[4]

programs.name: FAIR (Family Alzheimer's In-Home Respite)
programs.what_it_does: FAIR provides in-home respite care for families caring for individuals with Alzheimer's. It helps caregivers by offering temporary relief through state-supported services.[6]
programs.who_its_for: Families and caregivers of seniors with Alzheimer's disease.[6]
programs.whats_unique: State-funded respite program tailored to Alzheimer's care, administered by the Bureau of Senior Services, not a universal federal offering.[6]
programs.benefit_value: In-home respite hours (eligibility and amount determined by program guidelines).[6]
